concept: Open adapted external material / Guest register
description: Opens a marked external atom whose payload has been adapted to Phi pronunciation and romanization. The payload remains a guest rather than a lexicon word: the frame lets a speaker carry a personal name or external term without pretending that Phi has analyzed or adopted it.
sound_symbolism: The open vowel of the first syllable makes room for material arriving from beyond the core lexicon. The soft fricative of the second syllable carries that material gently, while final a opens toward the guest payload.
grammatical_notes: Always paired with the required closer hasho. The complete frame behaves as one nominal atom and may fill a noun phrase position or stand before nai as a predicate. Phi particles remain outside the frame. Guest payload follows Phi segmental and open-syllable constraints but may repeat syllables and need not be a lexicon entry. Every occurrence remains framed; adoption into the core lexicon requires a separate word-creation decision.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Communities can discuss local people, places, practices, and technologies while keeping Phi's core vocabulary deliberate and shared.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The boundary distinguishes accommodation from appropriation and makes the source status of a term audible before it is used.
